title: Amauropelta proctorii
intro: Amauropelta proctorii, also known as Jamaican ironwood, is a species of fern native to the Caribbean, particularly in Jamaica, and is a member of the Blechnaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may aid in wound healing and reduce pain. Bioactive constituents of Amauropelta proctorii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with Amauropelta proctorii include infusions and decoctions, which are often used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Amauropelta proctorii belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Amauropelta.
